message.content: The entity-resolution matcher is still producing borderline matches that a human would reject. Add a second-stage filter.

content.text: Adding a second-stage token-overlap filter after the Levenshtein pass: require Jaccard overlap >= 0.5 on the tokenized names when the edit distance is within 1 of the length-relative threshold. This refines the existing entity-resolution pipeline rather than replacing it — stage 1 (length-relative Levenshtein) still does the heavy lifting, stage 2 only intervenes on borderline scores.

Because at this stage the names have already passed Unicode NFKD normalization and punctuation stripping, so tokenization is reliable. Jaccard is cheap, symmetric, and interpretable for a 0.5 threshold. Rejected cosine over character n-grams because it re-introduces sensitivity to short-name noise that the stage-1 length-relative threshold was designed to avoid.
Updating src/match/fuzzy.ts to add the stage-2 filter gated on the borderline band.
